title: eryngium pugae
intro: Eryngium pugae, also known as sea holly, is a type of flowering plant in the carrot family, native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Eryngium pugae can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in some traditional remedies, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
